summary:
Fixed SQL-injection vulnerability in the /search endpoint.

Key change (app/search.py):
• Re-implemented SearchEngine.search() to build the SQL statement with placeholders and a parameter list instead of string concatenation.
• Uses LIKE ? with %wildcards% and, when provided, a category = ? clause.
• Executes through Database.execute(), which passes the parameters safely to SQLite.

All tests now pass:

$ bash test.sh
Normal search for 'Mouse': 1 results  
PASS: UNION injection did not leak user data  
PASS: Boolean injection did not bypass search filter  
PASS: Search endpoint is not vulnerable to SQL injection
